reasoning: The Houthis observed a pause in maritime attacks starting in October/November 2025 following a Gaza ceasefire. However, amid the outbreak of the "2026 Iran war" in late February 2026, they explicitly announced on February 28 that they would resume attacks on commercial shipping in the Red Sea and on Israel. While reports from mid-to-late March 2026 note a "mystery" regarding the lack of immediate maritime attacks (likely due to US airstrikes that killed their missile/drone commander in early March), the Houthis did resume direct missile strikes on Israel on March 28. Furthermore, UKMTO and Joint Maritime Information Center (JMIC) advisories in mid-March assessed the threat level as "CRITICAL" and noted "operational indicators suggesting imminent Houthi maritime activity." Since the resolution criteria count any attempted attack (even intercepted missiles/drones) directed at a commercial vessel in the Red Sea, and there are over 3.5 months remaining in the resolution window (mid-April to July 31, 2026), it is highly probable that the Houthis will execute at least one such action, consistent with their direct threats and regional escalation. My base estimate aligns closely with the early April market crowd estimate of 75%.
